auction_style: Spew are experienced, opportunistic drafters who emphasize speed/steals and hitting depth. They are willing to make big trades (acquired Pedro Strop/Torii Hunter block, gave away Arenado in a 2013 deal) and have shown a preference for balanced rosters over stars-and-scrubs. Andrew Lombardi handles most day-to-day communication; Brian Garber is quieter but strategically engaged. They are serial champions (2003, 2004, 2013, 2020, 2021, 2025) suggesting consistently strong draft execution.
price_tendency_detail: No direct auction price data available. Historical evidence suggests they get value at OF and RP while not overspending on starting pitching — Lombardi has repeatedly and vocally complained that Wins is a bad category, suggesting they deprioritize win-heavy SPs.
trade_style: Moderately active traders. They make targeted moves to shore up category needs (speed, batting average). In 2013 they gave away young Arenado for veterans and won the title, suggesting they value proven contributors over prospects. Lombardi is vocal in trades — he pushed back hard on a 2019 Chathams offer, accusing them of poor form, showing he negotiates from conviction.
notable_tendencies: Andrew Lombardi is the vocal personality — quick-witted, self-deprecating humor, handles all league communication
Brian Garber is the quieter strategist, based in Arizona; team has a bi-coastal dynamic
Loudest critics of the Wins category — Lombardi has repeatedly pushed for Quality Starts as a replacement
6 championships (most in league history) — consistent performers across different eras
Lombardi also handles league finances as treasurer — positions him as a central league figure with extra intel
In 2013 they famously traded Nolan Arenado + Dempster + Moreland for Strop/Hunter/Rogers and won the title
intel_summary: Spew are the winningest franchise in Teddy Ballgame history, and they know it. Lombardi's finance role gives him information advantages on who has paid and who is distracted. They are not sentimental about young players and will deal prospects for proven pieces. Expect them to be aggressive early in the auction on OF depth and late-round value; they tend to underinvest in starting pitching and compensate with waiver pickups.
